title: Smoothed Pit-Window Hazard Encoding
summary: Race strategy guides describe compound-specific pit windows, and the data has repeated Race, Year, Compound, Stint, and TyreLife patterns.
rationale: Race strategy guides describe compound-specific pit windows, and the data has repeated Race, Year, Compound, Stint, and TyreLife patterns. A target-safe survival-style prior can encode when each compound/stint usually becomes pit-eligible.
implementation_hint: Build out-of-fold encodings using GroupKFold by Race or Race-Year: smoothed mean PitNextLap by Compound-Stint-TyreLife bucket, Race-Compound-Stint-TyreLife, and Driver-Compound-Stint. Add cumulative hazard up to current TyreLife and distance_to_peak_hazard. For test, fit encoders on full train with Bayesian smoothing/min-count fallback.
expected_effect: May improve AUC by giving trees a direct but leakage-controlled prior for scheduled pit windows.
risk: Random validation will overstate gains; encodings must be OOF for train rows and grouped to avoid memorizing race-specific labels.
